vcs_json_input_support.mechanism: The existing Verilog case2_tb.v reads tb/input.mem and tb/output.mem with $fscanf. A JSON/Z3 generator must first emit those two .mem files; then VCS runs the Verilog testbench normally and collects coverage.
vcs_json_input_support.important_detail: VCS does not directly parse JSON for this testbench.

public_dataset_run.command_shape: vcs -full64 -sverilog +v2k -timescale=1ns/1ps +vcs+initreg+random -cm line+branch -cm_dir coverage.vdb -f filelist.txt -o simv -l comp.log; ./simv +vcs+initreg+0 -cm line+branch -cm_dir coverage.vdb -l sim.log; urg1 -dir coverage.vdb -format text -report report

scoreboard.write_update_rule: each cycle loads rst, s_axis fields, and m_axis_tready from input.mem
scoreboard.read_compare_rule: after the clock edge, s_axis_tready and all m_axis fields are compared against output.mem
scoreboard.pass_rule: all 7403 vector cycles match and both vector files end exactly at CYCLE_COUNT
